transformations: Moved the generic IR and printer from the mixed compiler class into independent modules.
Renamed PhpCoreExpr, PhpCoreStmt, and PhpCoreArrayEntry to PhpExpr, PhpStmt, and PhpArrayEntry while retaining constructor names for migration clarity.
Removed the raw PHP block statement from the generic IR.
Added fail-closed validation for identifiers, qualified names, magic constants, and binary operators.
Made deterministic printer entry points explicit public package APIs.
Replaced WPHX and WordPress fixture names with neutral compiler fixtures.

features: validated structural PHP identifiers, qualified names, signature types, and relative paths
file, namespace, function, class, interface, trait, property, and method IR
typed parameters, by-reference boundaries, callable arrays, closures, and while control flow
deterministic declaration ordering with defensive IR array copies
immutable Haxe source ranges paired with generated declaration line ranges
exact PHP 7.4.33 and PHP 8.4.7 lint/runtime matrix
excluded: Reflaxe driver registration and typed-Haxe AST lowering
serialized PHP source maps and trace CLI

features: content-authenticated logical source identities with exact half-open UTF-8 byte ranges
semantic node identities for mapped declarations, members, and explicit statements
deterministic generated byte spans and emitter-owned line-trace anchors
canonical caller-named range-map serialization with fail-closed coordinate, overlap, tie, and content validation
neutral multibyte source-correlation fixture on exact PHP 7.4.33 and PHP 8.4.7
boundary: public WordPressHx map identity and package source index remain in compiler/wordpress
the Haxe and Genes trace CLI remains in packages/cli
no WordPress symbol, path, profile branch, or sibling checkout is a generic compiler input
excluded: browser Source Map v3 correlation owned by wordpresshx-sdk-034
arbitrary-Haxe typed-AST lowering and runtime/stdlib ownership

excludedResponsibilities: WordPress adapter registry and adapter bodies
full-port Haxe bootstrap and Core linker assumptions
WordPress template and direct-script segment plans
full-port emission manifest and ownership records
unproven Reflaxe driver and Haxe runtime/stdlib ownership
